start_new_user_welcome:
Hello! 👋
Welcome to the Learn Russian bot 🇷🇺

Here you can:
🧠 Learn useful words and phrases
🎮 Play fun mini-games
🎓 Take short, interactive lessons
🔊 Listen to real pronunciation

Please choose your language to start:
start_existing_user_welcome:
⬆️ Let's improve your Russian by chatting

Select an option from the menu:
game_cancelled_success: ✅ Your game progress has been cancelled.
no_active_game_to_cancel: You don't have an active game to cancel.
game_not_found_error: Sorry, this game could not be found.
active_game_exists_error: You already have an active game. Please finish or /cancel it first.
game_start_error: An error occurred while starting the game: {error}
game_logic_not_found_error: Error: Could not find the game logic.
voice_input_not_supported: This game doesn't support voice input.
start_speech_game_prompt: To practice your speech, please start a 'Speech Practice' game from the /menu.
game_uses_buttons_prompt: This game is controlled by buttons. To exit, use the /cancel command.
session_expired_menu_text: Your gaming session seems to have expired. Please start over from /menu.
session_expired_alert: It seems your game session has expired. Please start a new one.
game_russian_tutor_start_text:
Hello! I am your personal AI tutor for the Russian language.

You can ask me any questions about grammar, ask me to check your text, or just chat for practice.

Type your first message to begin. To end the lesson, send the /menu command.
game_russian_tutor_resume_text: Let's continue our Russian lesson! I'm ready for your questions.
game_russian_tutor_callback_text: This game is controlled by text messages.
game_russian_tutor_end_text:
Lesson finished! I hope you've learned something new. Come back whenever you want to practice more!

To choose another game, use the /menu command.
game_speech_practice_resume_text: Ok, let's continue your speech practice!
game_speech_practice_choose_category: Please choose a category for speech practice:
game_speech_practice_record_hint: Press and hold the 🎙 microphone button to record your voice.
menu_hint: To select a different game, send /menu.
game_speech_practice_feedback_good_try:
🤔 Good try! Here's the target:
`{target}`

Here's what I heard:
`{recognized}`
game_speech_practice_feedback_unrecogniz: 😕 Sorry, I couldn't understand what you said. Please try again.
game_speech_practice_end_text:
🎉 **Speech Practice Complete!** 🎉

You pronounced **{score}** out of **{total}** items correctly.

Amazing work! Keep practicing!
Choose another category or game from the /menu.
game_tw_prompt_from_ru: Translate this word to your language:
game_tw_feedback_incorrect:
❌ Not quite. The correct translation is:

*{pair}*
game_tw_end_text:
🎉 **Round Complete!** 🎉

Category: **{category}**
Your score: **{score}** out of **{total}**.

Choose another category or game from the /menu.
game_va_theory:
📝 **Quick Theory: Imperfective vs. Perfective**

• **IMPERFECTIVE (НСВ)**
❓ *Question:* что делать?
🔄 *Focus:* Process, repetition, duration.

• **PERFECTIVE (СВ)**
❓ *Question:* что сделать?
🏁 *Focus:* Result, completion, single action.

Context is key!
game_va_resume: Ok, let's continue with the Verb Aspect test!
game_va_already_answered: This question has already been answered.
game_va_feedback_incorrect:
❌ Not quite. The correct answer is *{correct}*.

_{explanation}_

game_phonetics_button_menu: Back to the list of lessons
game_phonetics_listen_prompt1_1: There are 10 vowels in Russian: а, у, э, о, ы, я, и, ю, е, ё.
game_phonetics_listen_prompt1_2:
а sounds like 'a' in ‘car’
у sounds like 'oo' in ‘cool’
э sounds  like 'e' in ‘end’
о sounds like 'o' in ‘more’
game_phonetics_listen_prompt1_3:
а – у – э – о

о – э – у – а

а – о      о – у      у – э

о – э      э – а       а – у
game_phonetics_hint1_3: Listen to the audio & repeat out loud
game_phonetics_listen_prompt2_1: Sound of the Russian letter ‘ы’ has no equivalent in English. To pronounce ‘ы’, pull your tongue back in your mouth and try to say ‘ill’. The sound should be coming from deep down in your throat. The position of the tongue is similar to the position for the Russian vowel ‘y’, but the lips remain neutral and relaxed — not protruded or rounded.
game_phonetics_listen_prompt2_2:
ы – у      у – ы 

а – э – о – у – ы
game_phonetics_hint2_1: Listen to the audio and repeat
game_phonetics_hint2_2: Listen to the audio & repeat out loud
game_phonetics_hint2_3: Listen to the audio and repeat
game_phonetics_listen_prompt3_1:
с sounds like 's' in ‘sun’
к sounds like 'k' in ‘key’
м sounds like 'm' in ‘man’
game_phonetics_listen_prompt3_2:
са – су – сы – сэ – со

ка – ку – кы – кэ – ко

ма – му – мы – мэ – мо

ам – ум – ым – эм – ом
game_phonetics_listen_prompt3_3:
как   мак     ком       кум        ум       

эму     эмо       Эмма     мы       мыс

сам     сом        сок       сук       усы
game_phonetics_hint3_1: These three consonants are pronounced like their corresponding letters in English
game_phonetics_hint3_2: Listen to the audio & repeat the syllables out loud
game_phonetics_hint3_3: Listen to the audio & repeat the words. Then read the words out loud
game_phonetics_listen_prompt4_1:
The consonants ‘н’ and ‘л’ sound almost like the English letters ‘n’ and ‘l’ in the words ‘note’ and ‘love’. But, their pronunciation is a bit different. 

When you articulate the Russian letters ‘н’ and ‘л’, the tongue touches the back of the upper teeth.

As for their English counterparts, the tongue is a bit further back. It touches the teeth ridge – the bumpy area behind the upper front teeth.
game_phonetics_listen_prompt4_2:
на - ну - но - нэ - ны

ан - ун - он - эн - ын
game_phonetics_listen_prompt4_3:
нам     нас     нос     он        

кон     сон     сан     сын
game_phonetics_listen_prompt4_4:
ла - лу - ло - лэ - лы

на - ла     ну - лу    но - ло     нэ - лэ   ны - лы

ал - ул - ол - эл - ыл
game_phonetics_listen_prompt4_5:
лак        лук        лом      лоск

класс     клан      клон     клык

слон       слом     кол       мул
game_phonetics_hint4_1: Listen to the audio and repeat
game_phonetics_hint4_2: Listen to the audio & repeat out loud
game_phonetics_hint4_3: Listen to the audio & repeat the words out loud
game_phonetics_hint4_5: Listen to the audio & repeat the words out loud
game_phonetics_listen_prompt5_1:
Russian consonants ‘т’ and ‘д’ resemble the English letters ‘t’ and ‘d’, but the pronunciation is different.  

When pronouncing the Russian letters ‘т’ and ‘д’, you press the tip of the tongue against the back of the upper teeth, rather than against the teeth ridge, as it is with the English letters ‘t’ and ‘d’. This tongue position is the same when articulating the letter ‘n’ in ‘note’.
game_phonetics_listen_prompt5_2:
на - та - да      ну - ту - ду      но - то - до       нэ - тэ - дэ      ны - ты - ды

та - ту - то - тэ - ты

да - ду - до  - дэ - ды

та - да      ту - ду      то - до      тэ - дэ      ты - ды
game_phonetics_listen_prompt5_3:
так      там       тон        ты        тут

нут      кот        эта       сыт       мост    

стук     стул      стол      сток     стан

да        дан       дол       дом      дым
game_phonetics_hint5_1: Listen to the audio and repeat
game_phonetics_hint5_2: Listen to the audio & repeat the syllables
game_phonetics_hint5_3: Listen to the audio & repeat the words. Then read the words out loud
game_phonetics_listen_prompt6_1:
п sounds like 'p' in ‘pet’
б sounds like 'b' in ‘bed’
game_phonetics_listen_prompt6_2:
па - пу - по - пэ - пы

ба - бу - бо - бэ - бы

па - ба       пу - бу       по - бо       пэ - бэ       пы - бы

ап - уп - оп - эп - ып
game_phonetics_listen_prompt6_3:
пол       пыл        пуск       план

суп       сноп       топ         стоп     

бал       банк       босс       бокс      

бык       быт        бум        блок
game_phonetics_hint6_1:
п sounds like 'p' in ‘pet’
б sounds like 'b' in ‘bed’
game_phonetics_hint6_2: Listen to the audio & repeat the syllables
game_phonetics_hint6_3: Listen to the audio & repeat the words. Then read the words out loud
game_phonetics_listen_prompt7_1:
ф sounds like 'f' in ‘fun’
в sounds like 'v' in ‘very’
game_phonetics_listen_prompt7_2:
фа - ва       фу - ву       фо - во       фэ - вэ       фы - вы

аф - уф - оф - эф - ыф
game_phonetics_listen_prompt7_3:
вас       вам        вата        вы

вол       вот        внук        фут   

фон      фан       фанк       факс
game_phonetics_hint7_1: These consonants are pronounced like corresponding letters in English
game_phonetics_hint7_2: Listen to the audio & repeat the syllables
game_phonetics_hint7_3: Listen to the audio & repeat the words. Then read the words by yourself
game_phonetics_listen_prompt8_1: The Russian consonant ‘р’ resembles the English letter ‘r’ in the word ‘right’. But, unlike the English letter ‘r’, it is a vibrating sound. When articulating it, the tip of the tongue is raised to the teeth ridge, but is not bent back. The tip of the tongue vibrates slightly touching the teeth ridge.
game_phonetics_listen_prompt8_2:
ра - ру - ро - рэ - ры

пра - бра    пру - бру    про - бро    прэ - брэ     пры - бры 

ар - ур - ор - эр

ла - ра   ло - ро   лу - ру   лы - ры
game_phonetics_listen_prompt8_3:
рак       рок        ром      ромб       рэп

брак     бром      брас     брус       прок     брат    прут

бар       пар        вор       бор        дар

сыр       сэр        мэр       парк      Марс    

фарс     март      спорт
game_phonetics_hint8_1: Listen to the audio and repeat
game_phonetics_hint8_2: Listen to the audio & repeat the syllables
game_phonetics_hint8_3: Listen to the audio & repeat the words. Then read the words out loud
game_phonetics_listen_prompt9_1: Russian, like English and many other languages, uses word stresses. It means that one syllable in the word (the stressed one) is emphasized more than the rest. If there is only one syllable in the words, it is always stressed.
game_phonetics_listen_prompt9_2:
Stress is essential for correct pronunciation and spelling, because some vowel sounds in unstressed syllables are pronounced differently.

Moreover, the misplacement of the stress may even affect the change in the word's meaning. For example:

замо́к (lock) - за́мок (castle), плачу́ (I pay) - пла́чу (I cry), etc.
game_phonetics_listen_prompt9_3:
ма́ма          па́па            ка́рта          ры́ба          па́ста        фо́рма

рука́          сала́т           бана́н          бала́нс       балко́н      футбо́л       

рабо́та       буты́лка      капу́ста        банкно́та
game_phonetics_hint9_1:
Listen to the audio and read the examples:

ма́ма, па́ста, сала́т, бана́н, рабо́та
game_phonetics_hint9_2: Normally, texts in Russian are written without stress marks. But, stress marks are used in dictionaries and texts for beginning learners.
game_phonetics_hint9_3: Listen to the audio & repeat the words, paying attention to the stress. Then, read the words out loud
game_phonetics_listen_prompt10_1:
и sounds like 'ea' in ‘each’
е sounds like 'ye' in ‘yet’
я sounds like 'ya' in ‘yard’
ю sounds like 'u' in ‘use’
ё sounds like 'yo' in ‘yoga’

NOTE! The letter ‘ё’ is always stressed in Russian words. So, we don’t put the stress mark in words with ‘ё’.
game_phonetics_listen_prompt10_2:
и - е - я - ю - ё

ё - ю - я - е - и 

ян - ин - юн - ен - ён

ял - ил - юл - ел - ёл
game_phonetics_listen_prompt10_3:
икра       икона      поиск       если      евро

уеду       яма          ярус        туя         юла       

юрта       уют         ёлка        паёк       наём
game_phonetics_hint10_1: These vowels are pronounced the same as in English
game_phonetics_hint10_2: Listen to the audio & repeat out loud
game_phonetics_hint10_3: Listen to the audio & repeat the words. Then read the words out loud
